alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model REAL-FND incorporates auxiliary information to improve fake news detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model, REinforced Adaptive Learning Fake News Detection (REAL-FND), incorporates auxiliary information to improve fake news detection.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is essentially identical between preprint and publication, only expanding the acronym in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: REAL-FND demonstrates robustness in a target domain despite being trained in a different source domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: REAL-FND demonstrates robustness in a target domain despite being trained in a different source domain.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated the same way in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments illustrate the effectiveness of REAL-FND, particularly with limited labeled data in the target domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on real-world datasets indicate the effectiveness of REAL-FND, particularly when limited labeled data is available in the target domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier "on real-world datasets" (a clarification) and downgrades certainty to medium, so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our rule-based system outperforms the two other models in nearly all evaluations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our rule-based system outperforms the two other models in nearly all evaluations.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our system provides significantly higher Recall compared to the Machine Learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our system provides significantly higher Recall compared to the Machine Learning models.
alignments.rationale: The recall advantage claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining rules and Machine Learning models into hybrid systems could potentially provide even better performances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining rules and Machine Learning models into hybrid systems could potentially provide even better performances.
alignments.rationale: The suggestion to combine rules and ML remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying Rule Learning algorithms could help adapt rule sets to different publication periods in historical documents.
alignments.rationale: The specific preprint suggestion about applying Rule Learning to adapt rule sets does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our system can be used to produce annotated data sets that are large enough to envisage Machine Learning or Deep Learning approaches for the task of Logical Layout Analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new claim about using the system to generate datasets for ML/Deep Learning that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel dataset consisting of manual annotations of temporal localization for 13,000 narrated actions in 1,200 video clips has been introduced.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel dataset consisting of manual annotations of temporal localization for 13,000 narrated actions in 1,200 video clips has been introduced.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is stated the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method localizes the narrated actions based on their expected duration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method localizes narrated actions based on their expected duration and brings complementary information compared to previous methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same localization mechanism but adds that it brings complementary information relative to prior methods, a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method shows improvements over previous work for the task of temporal action localization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method leads to improvements over previous work for the task of temporal action localization.
alignments.rationale: The improvement-over-prior-work claim is preserved with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of the dataset allows for a better understanding of the interaction between language and visual modalities in the videos.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of the dataset allows for a better understanding of the interaction between language and visual modalities in the videos.
alignments.rationale: The dataset analysis conclusion is presented the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cross-modal representation learning improves performance in a given task compared to using only one of the modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cross-modal representation learning improves performance in a given task compared to using only one of the modalities.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about cross-modal learning is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adapting the triplet loss for cross-modal representation learning achieves higher accuracy in time-series classification by utilizing information from image classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapting the triplet loss for cross-modal representation learning achieves higher accuracy in time-series classification by utilizing information from image classification.
alignments.rationale: The methodological result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed triplet loss with a dynamic margin enhances classification accuracy, convergence speed, and generalizability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed triplet loss with a dynamic margin enhances classification accuracy, convergence speed, and generalizability.
alignments.rationale: The described benefits of the triplet loss are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improved generalizability leads to better adaptability between writers for online handwriting recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improved generalizability leads to better adaptability between writers for online handwriting recognition.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about adaptability across writers is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aryl brings 1.53x and 1.50x reductions in average queuing time and job completion time (JCT), respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lyra brings 1.53x and 1.48x reductions in average queuing time and job completion time (JCT), respectively.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved but the system name changed and the JCT reduction value was slightly revised from 1.50x to 1.48x.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aryl improves cluster usage by up to 26.9% over the cluster scheduler without capacity loaning or elastic scaling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lyra improves cluster usage by up to 25%.
alignments.rationale: The improvement-in-usage claim is maintained but the reported maximum improvement value was revised downward and the system name changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aryl introduces capacity loaning to loan idle inference GPU servers for training jobs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lyra introduces capacity loaning to loan idle inference servers for training jobs.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution (capacity loaning of idle inference servers) remains, but the publication removes the explicit "GPU" qualifier, a slight clarification/generalization; the system name also changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aryl exploits elastic scaling that scales a training job's GPU allocation to better utilize loaned resources.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about elastic scaling of GPU allocations does not appear among the published claims, which instead emphasizes different management heuristics.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Lyra addresses combinatorial problems in cluster management with principled heuristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new high-level claim about addressing combinatorial cluster management problems with heuristics that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.rationale: The core claim of generating damaged responses is preserved but the publication softens wording from 'accurately generate' to producing 'possible responses', reducing expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transition of structural dynamic signature from an undamaged to damaged state can be employed for predictive damage detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution about understanding/generating damaged conditions while healthy with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach allows for a more proactive oversight of life-cycle performance and prediction of the remaining useful life of structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include claims about life-cycle oversight or remaining useful life prediction present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The active form of gathering training data in Neural Galerkin schemes is key for numerically realizing the expressive power of networks in high dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments demonstrate that Neural Galerkin schemes can enable simulating phenomena and processes with many variables for which traditional and other deep-learning-based solvers fail. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural Galerkin schemes are particularly effective for problems where features of the solutions evolve locally, such as in high-dimensional wave propagation problems and interacting particle systems. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed two-stage weighted K asynchronous FL with adaptive learning rate (WKAFL) utilizes stale gradients and mitigates the impact of non-IID data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: WKAFL achieves multifaceted enhancement in training speed, prediction accuracy, and training stability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates efficiency in separating the ‘modes’ when tested on simulated data and experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian target-vector optimization scheme improves performance over previous developments in parameter reconstruction problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Bayesian target-vector optimization scheme is presented that improves performance over previous developments in parameter reconstruction problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim (presentation of a Bayesian scheme that improves performance); wording/role is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new optimization method outperforms established optimization methods for an optical metrology reconstruction problem and two synthetic least-squares problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms established optimization methods for an optical metrology reconstruction problem and two synthetic least-squares problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is reproduced in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method enables accurate uncertainty estimates with very few observations of the actual model function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method enables accurate uncertainty estimates with very few observations of the actual model function by using Markov chain Monte Carlo sampling on a trained surrogate model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific mechanism (MCMC on a trained surrogate) clarifying how the uncertainty estimates are obtained, so the claim is clarified without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning frameworks such as Genetic Programming and Reinforcement Learning are effective in flow control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning frameworks such as genetic programming and reinforcement learning are effective in flow control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about ML frameworks for flow control.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparative analysis benchmarks representative algorithms against global optimization techniques like Bayesian Optimization and Lipschitz global optimization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparative analysis of control algorithms illustrates differences in exploration versus exploitation and model capacity versus required complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the qualitative outcomes of the comparative analysis (exploration/exploitation, capacity/complexity) and omits explicit mention of benchmarking against specific global optimization methods, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The control algorithms were tested on three specific test cases, demonstrating their applicability to various flow control problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the 'three specific test cases' claim or equivalent; that specific testing detail is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparison illustrates differences in exploration versus exploitation and the balance between model capacity and required complexity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparative analysis of control algorithms illustrates differences in exploration versus exploitation and model capacity versus required complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this comparative finding but reports higher certainty (medium -> high), amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings pave the way toward the hybridization of various methods in flow control. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings of this work suggest a pathway towards the hybridization of various flow control methods. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same low-certainty conclusion about hybridization pathways.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian deep learning network 'DPNNet-Bayesian' can predict planet mass from disk gaps and provides uncertainties associated with the prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian deep-learning network, DPNNet-Bayesian, can predict planet mass from disk gaps and provides uncertainties associated with the prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the main result claim verbatim, with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DPNNet-Bayesian distinguishes between uncertainty associated with the deep learning architecture and uncertainty inherent in the input data due to measurement noise. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DPNNet-Bayesian distinguishes between uncertainty from the deep-learning architecture and uncertainty from input data due to measurement noise. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same, but the publication reports a higher certainty level (medium -> high), increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts planet masses of 86.0 ± 5.5 M_{\text{Earth}}, 43.8 ± 3.3 M_{\text{Earth}}, and 92.2 ± 5.1 M_{\text{Earth}} for dust gaps observed in HL Tau. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicts planet masses of 86.0 ± 5.5 M ⊕, 43.8 ± 3.3 M ⊕, and 92.2 ± 5.1 M ⊕ for the dust gaps observed in HL Tau. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric predictions and uncertainties are reproduced exactly, with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions made by DPNNet-Bayesian are comparable to other studies based on specialized simulations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions from DPNNet-Bayesian are comparable to those from other studies based on specialized simulations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative conclusion is preserved with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for embedding and training a general neural network in a quantum annealer without classical elements in training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general neural network can be embedded and trained in a quantum annealer without introducing any classical element in training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central contribution with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantum annealer trains the network by finding the ground state of the encoded loss function represented as an Ising model Hamiltonian. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows encoding the loss function as an Ising model Hamiltonian. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same technical mechanism (encoding the loss as an Ising Hamiltonian) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantum training method demonstrates consistency in finding the global minimum of the loss function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum training demonstrates consistency in finding the global minimum of the loss function and converges in a single annealing step. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim about consistent identification of the global minimum and augments it with an additional detail (single annealing step) but preserves the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network training converges in a single annealing step, leading to short training times while maintaining high classification performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training process leads to short training times while maintaining high classification performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about short training times and maintained performance but reports lower certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach opens a novel avenue for the quantum training of general machine learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach opens an avenue for the quantum training of general machine learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the concluding statement about opening an avenue for quantum training with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Flow-based algorithms may provide efficient sampling of field distributions for lattice field theory applications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Flow-based algorithms may provide efficient sampling of field distributions for lattice field theory applications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are word-for-word identical in content and qualification, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a numerical demonstration of robust flow-based sampling in the Schwinger model at the critical value of the fermion mass. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a numerical demonstration of robust flow-based sampling in the Schwinger model at the critical value of the fermion mass. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is identical between preprint and publication with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional methods fail to sample all parts of configuration space at the same parameters, leading to severely underestimated uncertainties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conventional methods fail to sample all parts of configuration space at the same parameters, leading to severely underestimated uncertainties. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about failures of conventional methods is unchanged in wording and implication between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose and implement an efficient and differentiable propagation model based on the Collins integral. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose and implement an efficient and differentiable propagation model based on the Collins integral. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution statement in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method enables the optimization of diffraction optical systems with unprecedented design freedom using backpropagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method enables the optimization of diffractive optical systems with unprecedented design freedom using backpropagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is equivalent; minor wording variation ('diffraction/diffractive') does not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the applicability of our method by engineering shift-variant PSFs via thin plate elements placed in arbitrary planes inside complex imaging systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the applicability of our method by engineering shift-variant PSFs via thin plate elements placed in arbitrary planes inside complex imaging systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching experimental demonstration claim with identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We perform cascaded optimization of multiple planes and design optimal machine-vision systems by deep learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We perform cascaded optimization of multiple planes and design optimal machine-vision systems by deep learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced artificial neural network architecture processes sensorimotor information and invokes highly flexible, goal-directed behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced artificial neural network architecture processes sensorimotor information and invokes highly flexible, goal-directed behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture develops latent states that can be interpreted as affordance maps, signaling which actions lead to which effects dependent on the local context. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture develops latent states that can be interpreted as affordance maps, signaling which actions lead to which effects dependent on the local context. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about latent states and affordance-map interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated agent can flexibly steer through continuous spaces, avoid collisions with obstacles, and prefer pathways that lead to the goal with high certainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulated agent can flexibly steer through continuous spaces, avoid collisions with obstacles, and prefer pathways that lead to the goal with high certainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Behavioral capabilities are described identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned agent demonstrates high suitability for zero-shot generalization across environments, performing similarly well in procedurally generated environments after training in fixed environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned agent demonstrates high suitability for zero-shot generalization across environments, performing similarly well in procedurally generated environments after training in fixed environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dynamic Mode Decomposition is an efficient technique for studying dynamic data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Dynamic Mode Decomposition is an efficient technique for studying dynamic data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is unchanged in wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal prediction approach based on the Mori-Zwanzig formalism can be applied to account for the effect of unresolved variables in dynamic systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal prediction approach based on the Mori-Zwanzig formalism can effectively account for the impact of missing data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes 'unresolved variables' as 'missing data', which narrows and alters the technical scope of what is being accounted for, constituting a revision rather than a simple clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method leads to a time-predictive model that effectively accounts for the impact of missing data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal prediction approach based on the Mori-Zwanzig formalism can effectively account for the impact of missing data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same substance about handling missing data but shifts emphasis to the approach itself; this is a clarification/small rephrasing rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first-order approximation of the Mori-Zwanzig decomposition provides a less computationally intensive solution while yielding similar dynamics to the exact decomposition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides a first-order approximation of the Mori-Zwanzig decomposition that is less computationally intensive than existing approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the combined computational-cost claim into a standalone claim about reduced cost (Q2) and a separate empirical claim about dynamics (Q3); the mapping to Q2 represents a clarified emphasis on computational savings while the publication also tones down certainty for the formal conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised CM-RSIR method does not require any annotated training images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised CM-RSIR method does not require any annotated training images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical statement about supervision requirements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method models mutual-information between different modalities in a self-supervised manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method models mutual-information between different modalities in a self-supervised manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method includes three loss functions that maximize mutual information, minimize angular distance, and increase cosine similarity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method includes three loss functions that maximize mutual information, minimize angular distance, and increase cosine similarity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The descriptive claim about loss components remains the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIDGN disentangles the user's intents from two different perspectives: global and local.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIDGN disentangles user’s intents from both a Global view and a Local view, enhancing the understanding of user preferences and item associations.
alignments.rationale: The publication's wording restates the same two-view disentanglement claim (Global and Local) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: From the global view, MIDGN demonstrates the user's preference under each intent more clearly by involving more items.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIDGN disentangles user’s intents from both a Global view and a Local view, enhancing the understanding of user preferences and item associations.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the global-view effect (better preference understanding) but omits the specific phrasing about "involving more items," making the statement somewhat more general.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: From the local view, MIDGN reveals the association among items under each intent since items within the same bundle are highly correlated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Multi-view Intent Disentangle Graph Networks (MIDGN) model effectively captures the diversity of user intent and item associations at a finer granularity.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same local-view idea as capturing item associations at finer granularity but uses broader phrasing and drops the specific "bundle" wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that MIDGN outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by over 10.7% and 26.8% on two benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that MIDGN outperforms state-of-the-art methods by over 10.7% and 26.8% on two benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: The numerical experimental results and datasets are reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Technology permits obtaining personal information without individuals' consent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Technology allows for the obtaining of personal information without individuals' consent.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim about technology enabling non-consensual acquisition of personal information with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computers make it feasible to share and process personal information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a strong interest in privacy issues in the XXIst century.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the discussion to broad interest in privacy (a higher-level framing) rather than the specific technical claim about computers enabling sharing/processing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biometric information is considered personal information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biometric information is considered personal information.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about biometrics being personal information.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are damaging implications associated with the unauthorized use of personal information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some claims about biometrics impinging upon individuals' privacy may not be true.
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies/skeptically reframes the preprint's assertion of damaging implications, shifting to a claim that some privacy-related assertions may be untrue.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The laws of observed trajectories can be determined using neural networks with a limited number of parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The laws of observed trajectories can be determined using neural networks with a limited number of parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about recovering laws with small-parameter neural networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training of the networks follows the Extreme Learning Machine idea.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training of the networks follows the extreme learning machine idea.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same training approach description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can successfully reconstruct both integrable and chaotic motions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can successfully reconstruct both integrable and chaotic motions, as demonstrated in the example of the gravity pendulum and the double pendulum.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the reconstruction claim with the demonstration examples but keeps the substantive result the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is demonstrated in the example of the gravity pendulum and the double pendulum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can successfully reconstruct both integrable and chaotic motions, as demonstrated in the example of the gravity pendulum and the double pendulum.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges the demonstration examples into a combined result claim without changing the content.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The laws can represent not only the equation of motion but also the symmetries of different kinds.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a novel claim about representation of symmetries that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DML-S2R method effectively mitigates the problems of insufficient labeled samples without the need for additional samples with target values.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DML-S2R method effectively mitigates the problems of insufficient labeled samples without the need for additional samples with target values.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim about addressing insufficient labeled samples without extra target-value samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DML-S2R employs a Siamese neural network to model pairwise sample similarities using a small number of labeled samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DML-S2R employs a pairwise similarity modeling approach using a small number of labeled samples to estimate target value differences.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same pairwise similarity modeling idea but uses slightly different terminology (pairwise similarity modeling vs. Siamese network).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method learns a triplet-based metric space that positions similar samples close together and dissimilar samples far apart, utilizing both labeled and unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes a triplet-based metric learning approach that incorporates both labeled and unlabeled samples to create a metric space.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the triplet-based metric learning approach with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the superiority of DML-S2R over existing state-of-the-art semi-supervised regression methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that DML-S2R outperforms state-of-the-art semi-supervised regression methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the experimental superiority claim with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning-based approach significantly improves traffic flow at urban intersections compared to traditional first-in-first-out schemes and static priority rules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates a significant gain in flow rate compared to a first-in-first-out scheme and traffic governed by static priority rules.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the empirical improvement claim against FIFO and static priority with the same substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned planner reduces the number of induced stops at urban intersections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned planner reduces the number of induced stops at urban intersections.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about reduced induced stops.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cooperative planning task is formulated as a reinforcement learning problem to learn a sensible policy without relying on expert demonstrations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can optimize traffic flow at urban intersections by jointly planning for multiple vehicles.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the contribution to ML-based joint planning for multiple vehicles; it drops the explicit RL formulation and the point about no expert demonstrations, broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A flexible graph-based input representation and a graph neural network are used to efficiently encode the scene and provide individual outputs for all involved vehicles.
alignments.rationale: The publication's presented claims do not mention the graph-based input representation or GNN architecture, indicating this preprint-specific technical detail was omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is evaluated using both synthetic simulations and real-world traffic data from the inD dataset.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation detail on datasets (synthetic + inD real-world data) appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regularized BLDA (RBLDA) achieves the best overall recognition performance on multivariate time series (MTS) data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBLDA outperforms RLDA on MTS data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts an absolute ‘best overall’ recognition performance while the paper reports RBLDA outperforming RLDA (a relative claim); this weakens/specificies the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model selection algorithm for RBLDA is efficient.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model selection algorithm is efficient.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same efficiency claim with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBLDA can produce better visualization of MTS data than regularized LDA (RLDA).
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBLDA can produce better visualization of MTS data than RLDA.
alignments.rationale: The visualization claim is preserved verbatim in the publication with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioADAPT-MRC can achieve state-of-the-art performance on benchmark biomedical-MRC datasets without using any synthetic or human-annotated data from the biomedical domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioADAPT-MRC can achieve state-of-the-art performance on benchmark biomedical-MRC datasets without using any synthetic or human-annotated data from the biomedical domain.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over exactly from preprint to publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direct-transferring of learned representations from a model trained on a general-purpose domain to the biomedical domain can hurt the model's performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a discrepancy in marginal distributions between the general-purpose and biomedical domains that can hurt model performance when transferring learned representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (marginal distribution discrepancy) behind the preprint's transfer-harm claim and reports it with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioADAPT-MRC relaxes the need for generating pseudo labels for training a well-performing biomedical-MRC model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioADAPT-MRC is an adversarial learning-based domain adaptation framework designed to address discrepancies in the marginal distributions between general and biomedical domain datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as an adversarial domain-adaptation framework addressing distributional discrepancies rather than explicitly emphasizing the relaxation of pseudo-label generation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reversible steganography is essential in fidelity-sensitive situations such as forensic science, legal proceedings, medical diagnosis, and military reconnaissance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reversible steganography is developed to address the inadmissibility of steganographic distortion in fidelity-sensitive situations.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core conclusion about the need for reversible steganography in fidelity-sensitive contexts with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study formulates reversible steganographic coding as a nonlinear discrete optimisation problem with a logarithmic capacity constraint and a quadratic distortion objective.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study formulates reversible steganographic coding as a nonlinear discrete optimisation problem with a logarithmic capacity constraint and a quadratic distortion objective.
alignments.rationale: The formulation claim is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results validate the near-optimality of the proposed optimisation algorithm when benchmarked against a brute-force method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results validate the near-optimality of the proposed optimisation algorithm when benchmarked against a brute-force method.
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional autoencoder with 12 hidden layers is able to reduce the dimensionality of the turbulence data to about 0.2% of their original size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional autoencoder with 12 hidden layers is able to reduce the dimensionality of the turbulence data to about 0.2% of their original size.
alignments.rationale: The dimensionality-reduction result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results indicate a fairly good accuracy in the first- and second-order statistics of the convective heat flux.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results indicate a fairly good accuracy in the first- and second-order statistics of the convective heat flux.
alignments.rationale: The statistical-accuracy claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm is able to reproduce the intermittent plume-mixing dynamics at the upper edges of the thermal boundary layers with some deviations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm is able to reproduce the intermittent plume-mixing dynamics at the upper edges of the thermal boundary layers with some deviations.
alignments.rationale: The reproduction-of-dynamics claim is maintained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the noise resilience of the framework which suggests the present model might be applicable as a reduced dynamical model for larger-scale computational models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The present model might be applicable as a reduced dynamical model that delivers transport fluxes and their variations to coarse grids of larger-scale computational models.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the noise-resilience and applicability points; this alignment matches the applicability portion of the preprint claim (both express potential applicability with low certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The global instance tracking (GIT) task is proposed to model the human visual tracking ability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The global instance tracking (GIT) task is proposed to model the human visual tracking ability by searching an arbitrary user-specified instance in a video without assumptions about camera or motion consistency.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit operational definition (searching arbitrary user-specified instances without camera/motion assumptions), thereby clarifying the original task proposal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high-quality and large-scale benchmark VideoCube has been constructed to create a challenging environment for tracking tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A high-quality and large-scale benchmark called VideoCube has been constructed to create a challenging environment for tracking tasks.
alignments.rationale: The benchmark construction claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A scientific evaluation procedure has been designed using human capabilities as the baseline to judge tracking intelligence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A scientific evaluation procedure has been designed using human capabilities as the baseline to judge tracking intelligence.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation-procedure claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate a definite gap between trackers and humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate a definite gap between trackers and humans in terms of tracking performance.
alignments.rationale: The result about a performance gap is restated in the publication with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The provided database, toolkit, evaluation server, and baseline results are available online.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate the availability/online-resources claim present in the preprint; thus this claim appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study develops a deep-learning network framework using the U-Net architecture with a Transformer block based fusion module to identify nerve tissues. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining birefringence images with deep learning can automatically detect nerves with gains upwards of 14% over color image-based (RGB) counterparts on the F2 score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims a specific architectural contribution while the publication frames the work as a general deep-learning + birefringence detection result; the method->result emphasis represents a revision in claim focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework leverages and extracts feature maps of each modality independently for cross-modal interactions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dual-modality framework achieves an F2 score of 76.12, which is a gain of 19.6% over single-modality networks using only RGB images. (certainty=high, type=result)
