alignments.rationale: Texts are effectively identical in scope and certainty, so the claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The OAML system combines the adaptation capabilities of online learners with the automated pipeline optimization capabilities of AutoML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The OAML system combines the adaptation capabilities of online learners with fast automated pipeline (re)optimization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same core contribution but adds emphasis on speed and (re)optimization; the stated certainty is reduced from high to medium, so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results confirm the utility of OAML over popular online learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results confirm the utility of OAML over popular online learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study underscores the benefits of continuous pipeline redesign in the presence of data drift. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study underscores the benefits of continuous pipeline redesign in the presence of data drift. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and certainty are the same in preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Building ensembles on the data level by combining different data augmentation methods produces classifiers that compete competitively against the state-of-the-art. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pretrained ResNet50 networks finetuned on training sets that include images derived from various augmentation methods demonstrate competitive performance against state-of-the-art classifiers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a general ensemble-level claim, while the publication reports competitive performance for finetuned ResNet50 models specifically, narrowing and revising the original claim and lowering certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifiers often surpass the best approaches reported in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Building ensembles on the data level by combining different data augmentation methods produces classifiers that often surpass the best approaches reported in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's result about ensembles often surpassing existing approaches with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three novel data augmentation methods based on the Fourier Transform, the Radon Transform, and the Discrete Cosine Transform are proposed for the first time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data augmentation methods based on the Fourier transform, Radon transform, and discrete cosine transform are effective in creating ensembles of neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the three proposed methods but shifts emphasis toward their effectiveness in ensembles rather than asserting ‘‘for the first time,’’ so the contribution is clarified while keeping similar scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model Unified-QG can continually learn QG tasks across different datasets and formats. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model Unified-QG can continually learn QG tasks across different datasets and formats. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core claim is carried over intact; only the label (conclusion vs contribution) differs, not the substance or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unified-QG effectively adapts to QG tasks when datasets and formats vary. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unified-QG effectively and continually adapts to QG tasks when datasets and formats vary. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the word ‘‘continually,’’ clarifying that adaptation is ongoing; the substantive claim and certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A single trained Unified-QG model improves the performance of 8 Question Answering (QA) systems by generating synthetic QA data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A single trained Unified-QG model improves the performance of 8 Question Answering (QA) systems by generating synthetic QA data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific quantitative result with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing QG methods are limited by their dedication to specific QG formats and optimal performance on only the datasets they were trained on. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing QG methods are limited by their dedication to specific QG formats and optimal performance on only the datasets they were trained on. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is unchanged in wording and certainty between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The encoder-decoder convolutional neural network effectively predicts the failure locations of porous metal tension specimens based on their initial porosities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The encoder–decoder convolutional neural network effectively predicts the failure locations of porous metal tension specimens based on their initial porosities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data- and loss-based regularization methods improve the prediction of failure locations in the presence of class imbalance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data- and loss-based regularization methods improve the prediction of failure locations in the presence of class imbalance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim with same certainty, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variational inference provides uncertainties for the neural network predictions, which are sensitive to the configuration of voids. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variational inference provides uncertainties for the neural network predictions, which are sensitive to the configuration of voids. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates this result unchanged in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted variances from the model are effective in ranking the locations that are most likely to fail in any given specimen. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted variances from the model are effective in ranking the locations that are most likely to fail in any given specimen. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is preserved exactly in the publication with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach applies classic ideas from linguistics to translate natural-language sentences into executable tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach applies classic ideas from linguistics to translate natural-language sentences into executable tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper explores the application of combinatory categorial grammars (CCGs) to generating property-based tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper explores the application of combinatory categorial grammars (CCGs) to generating property-based tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in both versions with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prototype is able to generate tests from English descriptions for each example in a textbook chapter on property-based testing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prototype is able to generate tests from English descriptions for each example in a textbook chapter on property-based testing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced verbatim in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoMC is an effective automatic tool for model compression that simplifies the selection of suitable compression methods for non-expert users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoMC is an effective and efficient automatic tool for model compression.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (AutoMC as an effective automatic compression tool) with similar emphasis; wording adds 'efficient' but does not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoMC builds domain knowledge on model compression to understand the characteristics and advantages of each compression method under different settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoMC improves search efficiency and quality by building domain knowledge on model compression.
alignments.rationale: The publication links the domain-knowledge building explicitly to improvements in search efficiency/quality, clarifying the intended benefit of the original claim without changing its focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoMC employs a progressive search strategy to efficiently explore pareto optimal compression schemes based on learned prior knowledge and historical evaluation information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The progressive search strategy in AutoMC helps selectively and gradually explore more valuable search space.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the progressive search strategy; the publication phrases it as selective/gradual exploration of valuable search space, a slightly narrower/less technical framing than the preprint's pareto-optimal emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results show that AutoMC can provide satisfying compression schemes within a short time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results show that AutoMC provides better compression schemes within a short time compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the empirical claim by adding an explicit comparative improvement over existing methods, changing the claim from ‘satisfying’ to demonstrably better.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieves a 0.962 validation F2 score and a 0.932 F2 score on test data from Guyana and Suriname.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieves a 0.962 validation F2 score and a 0.932 F2 score on test data from Guyana and Suriname.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact quantitative performance claim with identical scores.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental model trained on segmented cirrus images provides a statistically significant decrease in train time compared to the control model trained on raw cirrus images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Experimental model trained on the segmented cirrus images provides a statistically significant decrease in train time compared to the Control model trained on raw cirrus images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same experimental train-time reduction claim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature engineering can improve the performance of wildfire detection models by lowering computational expense.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature engineering can improve the performance of wildfire detection models by lowering computational expense.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about feature engineering and reduced computational expense is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed SEN12MS-CR-TS data set addresses the challenge of optical satellite image reconstruction and cloud removal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed SEN12MS-CR-TS data set addresses the challenge of optical satellite image reconstruction and cloud removal.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is carried over verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-modal multi-temporal 3D-Convolution Neural Network can predict a cloud-free image from a sequence of cloudy optical and radar images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multimodal multitemporal 3-D convolution neural network can predict a cloud-free image from a sequence of cloudy optical and radar images.
alignments.rationale: The network capability claim is preserved with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sequence-to-sequence translation model can predict a cloud-free time series from a cloud-covered time series.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sequence-to-sequence translation model can predict a cloud-free time series from a cloud-covered time series.
alignments.rationale: The sequence-to-sequence time-series reconstruction claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experiments demonstrate the benefits of multi-modal and multi-temporal information in reconstructing noisy information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiments demonstrate the benefits of multimodal and multitemporal information in reconstructing noisy information.
alignments.rationale: The experimental conclusion remains the same in content, but the publication indicates a lower certainty level (medium vs high), so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data-driven deep learning approach replaces the computationally heavy finite element calculations with a deep neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A data-driven deep learning approach can replace computationally heavy finite element calculations in the design phase of an electrical machine.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the replacement claim more specifically (in the design phase of electrical machines), clarifying and narrowing the original broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results indicate that the predictions of intermediate measures and the subsequent computations of key performance indicators are close to the ground truth for new machine designs.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the preprint's specific assertion about prediction closeness to ground truth for new designs; that experimental-accuracy claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid approach yields flexibility in the simulation process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid approach reduces computational time while maintaining high flexibility in the simulation workflow.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the flexibility claim but expands it by explicitly reporting computational time reduction alongside maintained flexibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid approach is quantitatively compared to existing deep neural network-based direct prediction approaches of key performance indicators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid approach is compared to the existing direct prediction approach for key performance indicators.
alignments.rationale: The comparison claim is present in both; the publication down-weights certainty (medium) relative to the preprint (high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network is trained using a large volume of stored finite element data in a supervised manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit claim about the supervised training procedure and dataset size that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extension of neural implicit surfaces to the space-time $\text{R}^3 \times \text{R}$ enables continuous geometric transformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extension of neural implicit surfaces to the space-time ℝ3 × ℝ enables continuous geometric transformations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the extension claim with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network training incorporates a data term that fits the initial condition to the corresponding time instant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network training incorporates a data term and a LSE term to fit the initial condition and approximate geometric evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the data-term claim but adds the LSE term and its role, clarifying and broadening the description of the training objective.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LSE term in the network training approximates the underlying geometric evolution without supervision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network training incorporates a data term and a LSE term to fit the initial condition and approximate geometric evolution.
alignments.rationale: The role of the LSE term in approximating geometric evolution is preserved verbatim in the publication's description of the training terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initializing the network based on previously trained initial conditions results in faster convergence compared to the standard approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Initializing the network based on previously trained initial conditions leads to faster convergence compared to the standard approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same claim about initialization improving convergence with the same level of specificity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Smooth neural networks can model dynamic variations of implicit surfaces under the level set equation.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts an additional modeling claim (smooth neural networks modeling level-set-driven dynamic variations) that was not explicitly present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Shuffled Frog Leaping Algorithm (SFLA) is a widely used population-based metaheuristic algorithm that combines memetics with particle swarm optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Shuffled Frog Leaping Algorithm (SFLA) is a widely used population-based metaheuristic algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim that SFLA is widely used but drops the specific detail about combining memetics with PSO, making the statement less specific (narrowed/clarified).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many improvements have been made to the SFLA to address its drawbacks through modifications or hybridizations with other algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many improvements have been made to the SFLA to address its drawbacks through modifications or hybridizations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides an overview of the SFLA, its recent modifications, hybridizations, and applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper provides a review of the most relevant works on SFLA, including its modifications, hybridizations, and applications.
alignments.rationale: Both texts assert the paper is a review of SFLA work, listing modifications, hybridizations and applications with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An operational framework of SFLA and its variants is proposed to analyze their uses across different application cohorts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An operational framework of SFLA and its variants is proposed to analyze their uses across different applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution with very similar wording and intended scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future improvements to the SFLA are suggested to enhance its performance and application.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future improvements to the SFLA are suggested to enhance its performance and application.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the suggestion of future improvements in the same low-certainty manner.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reward respecting subtasks are much more likely to be useful in planning than eigenoptions, shortest path options based on bottleneck states, or reward-respecting options generated by the option-critic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reward respecting subtasks are much more likely to be useful in planning than eigenoptions, shortest path options based on bottleneck states, or reward-respecting options generated by the option-critic.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific empirical claim verbatim with the same certainty and role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reward respecting subtasks strongly constrain the space of options and provide a partial solution to the problem of option discovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reward respecting subtasks strongly constrain the space of options and provide a partial solution to the problem of option discovery.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same but the publication lowers the expressed certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Values, policies, options, and models can all be learned online and off-policy using standard algorithms and general value functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Values, policies, options, and models can all be learned online and off-policy using standard algorithms and general value functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological/result claim unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides significant performance improvements in speaker recognition compared to earlier work.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides significant performance improvements in speaker recognition compared to earlier work.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance improvement claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dialogue pretraining combined with the rejection mechanism yields a 27.10% equal error rate (EER) reduction in speaker recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dialogue pretraining combined with the rejection mechanism yields a 27.10% equal error rate (EER) reduction in speaker recognition.
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric improvement and mechanism are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Noisy unlabeled data can provide valuable information that can be exploited using self-supervised training methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noisy unlabeled data can provide valuable information that can be exploited using self-supervised training methods.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about noisy unlabeled data appears identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The supervisory information in dialogues between customers and smart-speaker devices is inherently noisy.
alignments.rationale: This explicit observation about dialog supervisory noise appears in the published version but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep metric variational autoencoder demonstrates effective conditional data generation by sampling in the latent space within each class cluster.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep metric variational autoencoder demonstrates effective conditional data generation by sampling new embeddings in the latent space within each class cluster.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result; wording differs only slightly ("new embeddings") without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach shows promising results for the conditional generation of pairs of images and tympanograms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach shows promising results for the conditional generation of pairs of images and tympanograms.
alignments.rationale: The claim about promising conditional generation results is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method will allow for efficient data augmentation of data from multi-modal sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method will allow for efficient data augmentation of data from multi-modal sources.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the conclusion about efficient multi-modal data augmentation with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study uses character statistics of five-letter words to determine the best three starting words for Wordle.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study determines the best three starting words for the game Wordle using character statistics of five-letter words.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core contribution with the same method and outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerous attempts have been made to find the best starting word and best strategy to solve the daily Wordle.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this contextual claim about prior attempts; it is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The goal of the game is to guess a five-letter English word within six tries.
alignments.rationale: The basic game-rule statement from the preprint is not included among the publication's listed claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed starting words perform better than the currently available suggested word configurations.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts an empirical performance claim about the proposed words that was not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed starting words is validated across three distinct data sets, including two from the official Wordle word database.
alignments.rationale: This validation detail appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can learn to generate high quality portrait drawings in multiple styles using a single network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can learn to generate high quality portrait drawings in multiple styles using a single network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are identical in content and certainty about this result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can generate portrait drawings in a 'new style' unseen in the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can generate portrait drawings in a 'new style' unseen in the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about generating unseen styles is word-for-word the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel asymmetric cycle mapping that enforces the reconstruction information to be visible and only embedded in the selected facial regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel asymmetric cycle mapping that enforces the reconstruction information to be visible and only embedded in the selected facial regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method well preserves all important facial features in the generated drawings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method well preserves all important facial features in the generated drawings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Preservation of facial features is asserted the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments including a user study show that our model outperforms state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments including a user study show that our model outperforms state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Boolean observation games are a subclass of multi-player finite strategic games with incomplete information and qualitative objectives. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Boolean observation games are a generalization of Boolean games, which are a well-studied subclass of strategic games with complete information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The characterization changed from describing a subclass with incomplete information to framing the model as a generalization of Boolean games (complete-information subclass), so the conceptual relationship was revised and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nash equilibria are guaranteed to exist under certain conditions given an outcome relation in Boolean observation games. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify conditions under which, given an outcome relation, Nash equilibria are guaranteed to exist in Boolean observation games. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same result about identification of conditions guaranteeing existence of Nash equilibria.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The complexity of checking for the existence of Nash equilibria and verifying if a strategy profile is a Nash equilibrium is studied in the context of Boolean observation games. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complexity of checking for the existence of Nash equilibria and verifying if a strategy profile is a Nash equilibrium is studied in the context of Boolean observation games. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The complexity-analysis claim is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each Boolean observation game with 'knowing whether' goal formulas corresponds to a Boolean game and vice versa, with precise correspondences in terms of the existence of Nash equilibria. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each Boolean observation game with 'knowing whether' goal formulas corresponds to a Boolean game and vice versa, with precise correspondences in terms of the existence of Nash equilibria. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The correspondence between the two game classes is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ECRECer delivers the highest performance in predicting EC numbers, improving accuracy and F1 score by 70% and 20% over the state-of-the-art, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comparative analyses against 4 representative methods demonstrate that HDMLF stably delivers the highest performance, improving accuracy and F1 score by 60% and 40% over the state of the art, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert top performance over prior methods but the published version uses a different model name and reports different quantitative improvements, so the performance claim was revised (different numbers/name) though the overall claim of leading performance remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ECRECer can annotate numerous enzymes in the Swiss-Prot database with incomplete EC numbers to their full fourth level. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about annotating many Swiss-Prot enzymes to full fourth-level EC numbers appears in the publication; related platform/usage statements differ in focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction performance, usability, and efficiency of existing methods for predicting EC numbers still have much room to be improved. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The explicit limitation critique of existing methods is not present in the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-agent hierarchy deep learning-based framework learns the tasks in a multi-task manner for EC prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HDMLF, a hierarchical dual-core multitask learning framework, accurately predicts EC numbers based on novel deep learning techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's description of a hierarchical multi-task framework corresponds to the published hierarchical dual-core multitask framework; the publication presents a clearer, more specific architecture name and stronger performance framing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can also be used to uncover enzyme promiscuity, as shown in a case study of tyrB compensating for the loss of aspartate aminotransferase aspC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This case-study conclusion appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint's listed claims; therefore it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy improvement in traffic forecasting by developing new model structures is becoming marginal. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy improvement in traffic forecasting by developing new model structures is becoming marginal according to recent studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about marginal gains is preserved; the publication adds an attribution ('according to recent studies') but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Domain Adversarial Spatial-Temporal Network (DASTNet) framework can transfer forecasting-related knowledge across cities with different data distributions and network topologies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research is the first to employ adversarial multi-domain adaptation for network-wide traffic forecasting problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim about cross-city transfer is restated in the publication as employing adversarial multi-domain adaptation; the contribution is the same but expressed with more specific methodological framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DASTNet consistently outperforms all state-of-the-art baseline methods on three benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Domain Adversarial Spatial-Temporal Network (DASTNet) consistently outperforms all state-of-the-art baseline methods on three benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained DASTNet can deliver accurate traffic predictions immediately when new traffic detectors are available. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DASTNet can deliver accurate traffic predictions immediately (within one day) when new traffic detectors are available. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific timeframe ('within one day'), clarifying the original claim about immediate prediction capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study suggests an alternative approach to enhance traffic forecasting methods for cities lacking historical traffic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study suggests an alternative approach to enhance traffic forecasting methods for cities lacking historical traffic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding suggestion for cities without historical data is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural Language Generation (NLG) has improved significantly due to the development of sequence-to-sequence deep learning technologies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural Language Generation (NLG) has improved significantly due to the development of sequence-to-sequence deep learning technologies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same high-level conclusion about NLG improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning based generation is prone to hallucinate unintended text, which degrades system performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning based generation is prone to hallucinate unintended text, which degrades system performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Extensive Context Extraction Module (ECEM) improves accuracy by encoding multi-receptive-fields contextual information. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves comparable results to existing methods on the DroneRGBT dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a vague 'comparable' result with explicit quantitative outperforming claims, representing a substantive strengthening and broadening of the experimental claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Spatial Multi-Geometry FR (SMGFR) framework can reconstruct fluid flows around different two-dimensional objects without re-training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Spatial Multi-Geometry FR (SMGFR) framework can reconstruct fluid flows around different two-dimensional objects without the need for re-training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mapping approach in SMGFR improves percentage errors by up to 15% compared to conventional methods using a Cartesian grid. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mapping approach used in SMGFR improves percentage errors by up to 15% compared to conventional methods that train models on a Cartesian grid. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SMGFR framework achieves errors under 3%, 10%, and 30% for pressure, velocity, and vorticity fields predictions, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Spatio-temporal MGFR (STMGFR) task extends SMGFR to predict future snapshots of fluid flows. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel approach for STMGFR, which involves splitting DNNs into spatial and temporal components, reproduces the main features of flows around arbitrary objects in time and space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach for STMGFR, which involves splitting deep neural networks into spatial and temporal components, reproduces the main features of flows around arbitrary objects in time and space. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intercomparison of popular explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods reveals their fidelity in explaining CNN decisions for geoscientific applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study establishes an intercomparison of popular explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods to investigate their fidelity in explaining CNN decisions for geoscientific applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several important issues of XAI methods, such as gradient shattering and inability to distinguish the sign of attribution, have been overlooked in the field of geoscience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight several important issues of XAI methods, including gradient shattering, inability to distinguish the sign of attribution, and ignorance to zero input. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of XAI methods is expected to motivate further investigation into their fidelity and guide cautious implementation in geoscience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis aims to motivate further investigation into XAI fidelity and promote cautious implementation of XAI in geoscience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying XAI to a climate-related prediction setting demonstrates the need for careful consideration of XAI methods to avoid distorting the CNN decision-making strategy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If not considered cautiously, the identified issues may lead to a distorted picture of the CNN decision-making strategy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a correlation between flatness and generalization performance in deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In function space, flatter minima are closer to each other and the barriers along the geodesics connecting them are small. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In function space, flatter minima are closer to each other and the barriers along the geodesics connecting them are small. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Minimizers found by variants of gradient descent can be connected by zero-error paths composed of two straight lines in parameter space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Minimizers found by variants of gradient descent can be connected by zero-error paths composed of two straight lines in parameter space. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CECIILA offers novel methodologies for multiplexer, most significant bit, and modulus conversion operations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jury learning is a supervised machine learning approach that resolves label disagreements explicitly through the metaphor of a jury. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian uncertainty can be asymptotically obtained by the approximate message passing algorithm, bypassing the costly Monte Carlo sampling of the posterior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian uncertainty can be asymptotically obtained by the approximate message passing algorithm in high-dimensional Gaussian input data and labels generated by the probit model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is the same technical result presented in the publication, with matching scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A closed-form formula for the joint statistics between the logistic classifier, the uncertainty of the statistically optimal Bayesian classifier, and the ground-truth probit uncertainty is provided. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A closed-form formula for the joint statistics between the logistic classifier, the uncertainty of the statistically optimal Bayesian classifier, and the ground-truth probit uncertainty has been provided. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the closed-form joint-statistics formula as claimed in the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formula allows for the investigation of calibration of the logistic classifier learning from a limited amount of samples. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The formula allows for the investigation of the calibration of the logistic classifier when learning from a limited amount of samples. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the formula enables investigation of calibration under limited-sample conditions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over-confidence can be mitigated by appropriately regularising. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over-confidence in the model can be mitigated by appropriately regularizing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mitigation of over-confidence via regularization is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) is significant for learning high-level information about human activity from various devices. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a general claim about the overall significance of HAR; it focuses specifically on CNN-based approaches instead, so the broad preprint claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent works on HAR have utilized a wide range of deep neural network architectures, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNN-based approaches are suitable for effective and accurate human activity recognition system applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from describing usage of CNNs to asserting their suitability and accuracy for HAR — a stronger evaluative claim rather than a simple observation of usage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reviewed CNN architectures for HAR are categorized based on the type of input devices used. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding publication claim describes a device-based categorization of architectures, so this specific review result appears omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review discusses the performances, strengths, weaknesses, and hyperparameters of CNN architectures in HAR systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Challenges to CNN-based HAR systems include availability of data regarding composite or group activities, high computational resource requirements, data privacy concerns, and edge computing limitations. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the broad review discussion into a specific list of challenges/limitations; the content is therefore a narrower, more focused rendering with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current challenges and potential future directions for CNN-based HAR systems are identified in the review. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research should focus on more efficient edge computing techniques, datasets incorporating contextual information with activities, more explainable methodologies, and more robust systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's recommended future research directions closely mirror the preprint's stated challenges and future directions, with similar scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed framework offers both privacy guarantees for users and detection against poisoning attacks from them. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed framework offers both privacy guarantees for users and detection against poisoning attacks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same dual guarantees (privacy and poisoning detection) essentially verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new threat model is proposed that includes both an honest-but-curious server and malicious users. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new threat model is proposed that includes both an honest-but-curious server and malicious users. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The threat model is described identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The secure aggregation protocol uses homomorphic encryption to combine local model updates in a private manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The secure aggregation protocol uses homomorphic encryption to combine local model updates in a private manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same technical description of the secure aggregation protocol.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The zero-knowledge proof protocol allows the detection of attacks in the local models to be shifted from the server to the users. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The zero-knowledge proof protocol allows the detection of attacks in local models to be shifted from the server to the users. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this protocol role and its characterization unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework enables the central server to identify poisoned model updates without violating the privacy guarantees of secure aggregation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables the central server to identify poisoned model updates without violating privacy guarantees. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about identifying poisoned updates while preserving privacy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GLODISMO demonstrates improved performance in signal recovery applications compared to conventional designs based on randomization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned measurement matrices using GLODISMO outperform conventional designs based on randomization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim directly restates the preprint result that learned matrices outperform randomized designs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GLODISMO provides a computationally efficient method for learning discrete structured measurement operators. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GLODISMO is easy-to-implement, computationally efficient, and extendable due to its compatibility with automatic differentiation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the efficiency claim by attributing ease-of-implementation and extendability to autodiff compatibility, but with slightly lower reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method combines unrolled optimization with Gumbel reparametrizations to compute low-variance gradient estimates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GLODISMO demonstrates the ability to compute low-variance gradient estimates of categorical random variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the key result (ability to compute low-variance gradient estimates) derived from the described combination; this is a focused statement of the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GLODISMO is compatible with automatic differentiation, making it easy to implement and extend. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GLODISMO is easy-to-implement, computationally efficient, and extendable due to its compatibility with automatic differentiation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the autodiff compatibility claim but presents it with slightly lower confidence and couples it with ease/efficiency claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid Contrastive Quantization (HCQ) is the first quantized representation learning method for cross-view video retrieval. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hybrid Contrastive Quantization (HCQ) is the first quantized representation learning method for cross-view video retrieval. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this novelty claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HCQ learns both coarse-grained and fine-grained quantizations with transformers, preserving comprehensive semantic information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HCQ learns both coarse-grained and fine-grained quantizations with transformers, preserving comprehensive semantic information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this technical description without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that HCQ achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art non-compressed retrieval methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that HCQ achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art non-compressed retrieval methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HCQ shows high efficiency in storage and computation compared to existing text-video retrieval models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HCQ shows high efficiency in storage and computation compared to existing text-video retrieval models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same efficiency claim but reports it with reduced certainty (medium vs high), so the content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed surrogate fairness constraint called SLIDE is computationally feasible and asymptotically valid. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed surrogate fairness constraint called SLIDE is computationally feasible and asymptotically valid. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned model using SLIDE satisfies the fairness constraint asymptotically and achieves a fast convergence rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned model using SLIDE satisfies the fairness constraint asymptotically and achieves a fast convergence rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about asymptotic satisfaction and convergence is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments confirm that SLIDE works well for various benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments confirm that SLIDE works well for various benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental confirmation claim is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose an efficient attention layer based on the stochastic algorithm PatchMatch, referred to as a 'Patch-based Stochastic Attention Layer' (PSAL).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Patch-based Stochastic Attention Layer (PSAL) has a small memory footprint that allows it to scale to high resolution images.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core proposal of PSAL but presents it via its memory-scaling result rather than explicitly restating the PatchMatch-based algorithmic origin, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PSAL has a small memory footprint and can therefore scale to high resolution images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Patch-based Stochastic Attention Layer (PSAL) has a small memory footprint that allows it to scale to high resolution images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this exact result about memory footprint and scalability with high fidelity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PSAL maintains its small memory footprint without sacrificing spatial precision and globality of the nearest neighbors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PSAL maintains spatial precision and globality of the nearest neighbors without sacrificing memory efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same trade-off (maintaining spatial precision and global/globality while being memory-efficient) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the usefulness of PSAL on several image editing tasks, such as image inpainting, guided image colorization, and single-image super-resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usefulness of PSAL is demonstrated on several image editing tasks, including image inpainting, guided image colorization, and single-image super-resolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same application demonstrations and thus preserves the claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ensemble-based clustering strategy enables learning from unlabeled data even when samples from different classes are not prominently diverse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ensemble-based clustering strategy enables learning from unlabeled data in scenarios where classes have similar semantics.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability in slightly different wording (classes with similar semantics), preserving the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This strategy allows clusters with different densities and higher variability to emerge, reducing intra-class discrepancies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This strategy reduces intra-class discrepancies without the need for finding an optimal configuration per dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the reduction of intra-class discrepancies but frames it as no need for per-dataset configuration, which clarifies how the result is achieved/used.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method outperforms state-of-the-art methods with a fully-unsupervised solution without any labeling or human intervention.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both Person Re-Identification and Text Authorship Attribution tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the outperforming-SOTA claim but limits it to specific tasks (Person Re-ID and Text Authorship), narrowing the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: General self-supervised learning methods might fail to learn discriminative features in forensic applications where classes have similar semantics.
alignments.rationale: This cautionary/limitation statement from the preprint does not appear in the publication's claims list and is therefore removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms have proven to have high potential for processing bubbly flow images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms have proven to have high potential for processing bubbly flow images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about deep learning's potential for bubbly flow image processing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses the challenges of handling different image conditions and higher gas volume fractions using three methods based on Convolutional Neural Networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study addresses the challenges of handling different image conditions and higher gas volume fractions in bubble identification.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same contribution (addressing variable imaging conditions and higher gas fractions) though with slightly condensed wording; the meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology was validated using test data sets with synthetic images, demonstrating both capabilities and limitations of the combined approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology was validated using test data sets with synthetic images, demonstrating both capabilities and limitations.
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is carried over verbatim in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated data, code, and trained models are made accessible to facilitate further developments in bubble recognition research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated data, code, and trained models are made accessible to facilitate further developments in bubble recognition research.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same contribution about releasing data, code, and models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multimodal sensing approach enables simultaneous sensing of contact force, contact location, and temperature using a single soft material.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed optical high-resolution multimodal sensing approach enables the simultaneous sensing of three different physical quantities—contact force, contact location, and temperature—with a single soft material.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same multimodal sensing capability with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves spatially continuous sensing with ultrahigh resolution of few tens of micrometers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieves spatially continuous sensing with an ultrahigh resolution of few tens of micrometers, surpassing previous multimodal sensing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the resolution claim and adds that it surpasses prior methods, which clarifies and strengthens the original claim (amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of the proposed approach with a deep neural network allows for flexible selection of sensing modes based on purpose.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method does not require integrating multiple sensors, addressing the challenge of large-scale sensor integration.
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern system integration and flexibility; the publication reframes this as obviating multiple-sensor integration, which clarifies the integration benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach encourages the development of high-performance optical skins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A haptic soft device is presented for a human–machine interface, demonstrating the practical application of the proposed sensing approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete demonstration (haptic device) of the broader encouragement in the preprint, turning a general contribution into a demonstrated application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced polytope, called expohedron, represents all achievable exposures of items for a Position Based Model (PBM).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced polytope, called expohedron, represents all achievable exposures of items for a Position Based Model (PBM).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same definitional/conceptual contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Carathéodory decomposition algorithm has a complexity of $O(n^2\text{log}(n))$ and can express any point inside the expohedron as a convex sum of at most $n$ vertices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Carathéodory decomposition algorithm has a complexity of $O(n^2 log(n))$ and can express any point inside the expohedron as a convex sum of at most n vertices.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the algorithmic complexity and representational guarantee unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can recover the entire Pareto frontier of the multi-objective fairness-utility optimization problem with a complexity of $O(n^2\text{log}(n))$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach can recover the whole Pareto frontier of the multi-objective fairness-utility optimization problem with a complexity of $O(n^2 log(n))$.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same Pareto frontier recovery claim with equivalent complexity statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution can be expressed as a distribution over only $n$ permutations, which is more efficient than the $(n-1)^2 + 1$ permutations achieved with BvN decompositions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution can be expressed as a distribution over $\ndoc$ permutations, which is more efficient than the $(n-1)^2 + 1$ permutations achieved with BvN decompositions.
alignments.rationale: Despite minor corruption in the publication text, the intended claim matches the preprint: a solution using only n permutations is more efficient than the BvN bound.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets confirm the theoretical results of the proposed method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets confirm the theoretical results of the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the experimental confirmation claim unchanged.
